sections.label: Section 487
sections.original: καὶ τῶν νόμων γε μὴ πρότερον εἶναι θέλειν .
sections.translation: And another custom is to yield a willing deference to the laws .

sections.label: Section 488
sections.original: πᾶν τοὐξ ἀνάγκης δοῦλόν ἐστʼ ἐν τοῖς σοφοῖς .
sections.translation: The wise hold that everything which depends on necessity is a slave .

sections.label: Section 489
sections.original: κέκτησό νυν σὺ τοῦτʼ , ἐγὼ δʼ οὐ κτήσομαι .
sections.translation: Keep that wisdom for yourself ; I will not have it .

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ὀργὴ γὰρ ἅμα σου καὶ τὸ γῆρας οὐ σοφόν .
sections.translation: Yes , for you are angry , and also old age is not wise .

sections.label: Section 491
sections.original: πρὸς τόνδʼ ἀγὼν τίς ἀσοφίας ἥκει πέρι ;
sections.translation: What does a dispute about foolishness have to do with him ? If right and wrong are clear to all , who was ever more senseless than this man , because he never weighed the justice of the case ,

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἦλθεν ἐπὶ τὸν κοινὸν Ἑλλήνων νόμον ;
sections.translation: nor appealed to the universal law of Hellas ? For instance , when Agamemnon breathed his last struck on his head by my daughter a most foul deed , which I will never defend ,

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: χρῆν αὐτὸν ἐπιθεῖναι μὲν αἵματος δίκην ,
sections.translation: he should have brought a charge against his mother and inflicted a holy penalty for bloodshed , banishing her from his house ; thus he would have gained moderation instead of calamity , keeping strictly to the law and showing his piety as well . As it is , he has come into the same fate as his mother .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: κακὴν γὰρ αὐτὴν ἐνδίκως ἡγούμενος ,
sections.translation: for though he had just cause for thinking her a wicked woman , he has become more wicked by murdering her .

sections.label: Section 507
sections.original: ἐρήσομαι δέ , Μενέλεως , τοσόνδε σε ·
sections.translation: I will ask you , Menelaus , just one question . If a man ’ s wedded wife should kill him , and his son in turn will kill his mother in revenge ;

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: κἄπειθʼ ὁ κείνου γενόμενος φόνῳ φόνον
sections.translation: next the avenger ’ s son to expiate this murder will commit another : where will the chain of horrors end ?

sections.label: Section 512
sections.original: καλῶς ἔθεντο ταῦτα πατέρες οἱ πάλαι ·
sections.translation: Our forefathers settled these matters the right way . They forbade any one with blood upon his hands to appear in their sight or cross their path ;

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: φυγαῖσι δʼ ὁσιοῦν , ἀνταποκτείνειν δὲ μή .
sections.translation: but they purified him by exile , they did not kill him in revenge . Otherwise someone , by taking the pollution last upon his hands , is always going to be liable to have his own blood shed .

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ μισῶ μὲν γυναῖκας ἀνοσίους ,
sections.translation: Now I hate wicked women , especially my daughter who killed her husband ;

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: Ἑλένην τε , τὴν σὴν ἄλοχον , οὔποτʼ αἰνέσω
sections.translation: Helen , too , your own wife , I will never commend , nor would I even speak to her ; and I do not envy you a voyage to Troy for a worthless woman . But the law I will defend with all my might , to put an end to this brutal spirit of murder ,

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: παύων , ὃ καὶ γῆν καὶ πόλεις ὄλλυσʼ ἀεί .
sections.translation: which is always the ruin of countries and cities alike . Turning to Orestes Wretch ! Had you no heart when your mother was baring her breast in her appeal to you ? I , who did not see that awful deed , weep unhappy tears from my old eyes .

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: ἓν δʼ οὖν λόγοισι τοῖς ἐμοῖς ὁμορροθεῖ ·
sections.translation: One thing at least agrees with what I say : you are hated by the gods , and you pay atonement for your mother by your fits of madness and terror . Why do I need to hear from other witnesses what I can see for myself ? Therefore , Menelaus , take heed ;

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: μὴ πρᾶσσʼ ἐναντίʼ , ὠφελεῖν τοῦτον θέλων ,
sections.translation: do not oppose the gods in your wish to help this man ; but leave him to be stoned to death by the citizens , or do not set foot on Spartan land . My daughter is dead , and rightly ; but it should not have been his hand that slew her .

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ τἄλλα μακάριος πέφυκʼ ἀνήρ ,
sections.translation: In all except my daughters I have been a happy man ; there I am not blessed .

sections.label: Section 542
sections.original: ζηλωτὸς ὅστις εὐτύχησεν ἐς τέκνα
sections.translation: He is enviable , who is fortunate in his children , and does not bring hazardous notoriety on himself .

sections.label: Section 544
sections.original: ὦ γέρον , ἐγώ τοι πρὸς σὲ δειμαίνω λέγειν ,
sections.translation: Old man , I am afraid to speak before you ,

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: ὅπου σὲ μέλλω σήν τε λυπήσειν φρένα .
sections.translation: in a matter where I am sure to grieve you to the heart . I am unholy because I killed my mother , I know it , yet holy on another count , because I avenged my father . Only let your years , which frighten me from speaking , set no barrier in the path of my words ,

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: καὶ καθʼ ὁδὸν εἶμι · νῦν δὲ σὴν ταρβῶ τρίχα .
sections.translation: and I will go forward ; but now I fear your gray hairs .

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: τί χρῆν με δρᾶσαι ; δύο γὰρ ἀντίθες δυοῖν ·
sections.translation: What ought I to have done ? Set one thing against another . My father begot me ; your daughter gave me birth , being the field that received the seed from another ; for without a father no child would ever be born .

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: ἐλογισάμην οὖν τῷ γένους ἀρχηγέτῃ
sections.translation: So I reasoned that I ought to stand by the author of my being rather than the woman who undertook to rear me . Now your daughter — I am ashamed to call her mother — came to a man ’ s bed in a private and unchaste wedding ; I speak against myself when I speak

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: κακῶς ἐκείνην , ἐξερῶ · λέξω δʼ ὅμως .
sections.translation: badly of her , yet I will speak . Aegisthus was her secret husband in the home ; I killed him , and I sacrificed my mother , an unholy crime , no doubt , but done to avenge my father .

sections.label: Section 564
sections.original: ἐφʼ οἷς δʼ ἀπειλεῖς ὡς πετρωθῆναί με χρή ,
sections.translation: Now , as regards the matters for which I deserve to be stoned as you threatened ,

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: ἄκουσον ὡς ἅπασαν Ἑλλάδʼ ὠφελῶ .
sections.translation: hear the service I am conferring on all Hellas . For if women become so bold as to murder their husbands , taking refuge in their children , with the mother ’ s breast to catch the pity , they would think nothing of destroying their husbands

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: ἐπίκλημʼ ἐχούσαις ὅ τι τύχοι . δράσας δʼ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: on any charge whatsoever . But I , by a horrible crime , as you boast it to be , have put an end to this custom . I hated my mother and killed her justly . She was false to her husband when he was gone from his home to fight for all Hellas at the head of its armies ,

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: προύδωκε κοὐκ ἔσῳσʼ ἀκήρατον λέχος ·
sections.translation: and she did not keep his bed undefiled ; and when her sin had found her out , she did not impose punishment on herself , but , to avoid paying the penalty to her husband , punished my father by death . By the gods ! it is not a good time for me to mention the gods ,

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: φόνον δικάζων · εἰ δὲ δὴ τὰ μητέρος
sections.translation: when defending the charge of murder ; but if I consented by my silence to my mother ’ s conduct , what would the murdered man have done to me ? Would he not now in hatred be tormenting me with the Furies ? Or does my mother have goddesses as allies , but he does not , in his deeper wrong ?

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: σύ τοι φυτεύσας θυγατέρʼ , ὦ γέρον , κακὴν
sections.translation: You , yes ! you , old man , have been my ruin by begetting a wicked daughter ; for it was owing to her audacious deed that I lost my father and became my mother ’ s murderer .

sections.label: Section 588
sections.original: ὁρᾷς , Ὀδυσσέως ἄλοχον οὐ κατέκτανε
sections.translation: You see , Telemachus did not kill the wife of Odysseus , because she did not marry husband upon husband ,

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: μένει δʼ ἐν οἴκοις ὑγιὲς εὐνατήριον .
sections.translation: but the marriage-bed remained untainted in her home .

sections.label: Section 591
sections.original: ὁρᾷς δʼ Ἀπόλλωνʼ , ὃς μεσομφάλους ἕδρας
sections.translation: And you see Apollo , who makes the navel of the earth his home , dispensing to mortals unerring prophecies , whom we obey in all he says ; I killed my mother in obedience to him .

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: ἐκεῖνον ἡγεῖσθʼ ἀνόσιον καὶ κτείνετε ·
sections.translation: Find him guilty of the crime , slay him ; his was the sin , not mine . What ought I to have done ? or is the god not competent to expiate the pollution when I refer it to him ? Where then should anyone flee , if he will not rescue me from death after giving his commands ?

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὡς μὲν οὐκ εὖ μὴ λέγʼ εἴργασται τάδε ,
sections.translation: Do not say that the deed was done badly , but unfortunately for the ones who did it .

sections.label: Section 602
sections.original: γάμοι δʼ ὅσοις μὲν εὖ καθεστᾶσιν βροτῶν ,
sections.translation: A blessed life those mortals lead who make wise marriages ; but those for whom it does not fall out well are unfortunate in their public and private concerns .

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: αἰεὶ γυναῖκες ἐμποδὼν ταῖς συμφοραῖς
sections.translation: Women by nature always meddle in the doings of men , with unfortunate results .

sections.label: Section 607
sections.original: ἐπεὶ θρασύνῃ κοὐχ ὑποστέλλῃ λόγῳ ,
sections.translation: Since you are so bold and suppress nothing , but answer me back in such a way as to vex my heart , you will lead me to go to greater lengths in procuring your execution ;

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: καλὸν πάρεργον δʼ αὐτὸ θήσομαι πόνων
sections.translation: and I shall regard this as a fine addition to my labors in coming here to adorn my daughter ’ s grave . Yes , I will go to the chosen band of Argives and set the city , willing or not , on you and your sister , to pay the penalty of stoning .

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: μᾶλλον δʼ ἐκείνη σοῦ θανεῖν ἐστʼ ἀξία ,
sections.translation: She deserves to die even more than you , for it was she who embittered you against your mother , always carrying tales to your ear to increase your hate the more , announcing dreams from Agamemnon , and Aegisthus ’ bed ,

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: οἱ νέρτεροι θεοί · καὶ γὰρ ἐνθάδʼ ἦν πικρόν ·
sections.translation: may the gods in Hades loathe it ! for even here on earth it was bitter ; till she set the house ablaze with fires never kindled by Hephaestus .

sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: Μενέλαε , σοὶ δὲ τάδε λέγω δράσω τε πρός ·
sections.translation: Menelaus , I tell you this , and I will do it , too : if you then consider my hatred or our marriage connection of any account , do not ward off this man ’ s doom in defiance of the gods ,

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ἔα δʼ ὑπʼ ἀστῶν καταφονευθῆναι πέτροις ,
sections.translation: but leave him to be stoned to death by the citizens , or do not set foot on Spartan land . Remember you have been told all this , and do not choose the ungodly as friends , pushing aside the more righteous . Servants , lead me from this house . Exit Tyndareus .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: στεῖχʼ , ὡς ἀθορύβως οὑπιὼν ἡμῖν λόγος
sections.translation: Go , so that the remainder of my speech may come to this man without interruption , free from your old age .

sections.label: Section 632
sections.original: Μενέλαε , ποῖ σὸν πόδʼ ἐπὶ συννοίᾳ κυκλεῖς ,
sections.translation: Menelaus , why are you pacing round and round in thought , going back and forth , in a dilemma ?

sections.label: Section 634
sections.original: ἔασον · ἐν ἐμαυτῷ τι συννοούμενος
sections.translation: Let me alone ! When I think it over ,

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: ὅποι τράπωμαι τῆς τύχης ἀμηχανῶ .
sections.translation: I am perplexed to know where to turn in these events .

sections.label: Section 636
sections.original: μή νυν πέραινε τὴν δόκησιν , ἀλλʼ ἐμοὺς
sections.translation: Do not come to a final decision now , but after first hearing what I have to say , then make up your mind .

sections.label: Section 638
sections.original: λέγʼ · εὖ γὰρ εἶπας · ἔστι δʼ οὗ σιγὴ λόγου
sections.translation: Good advice ! Speak . There are times when silence would be better than speech , and the reverse also .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: λέγοιμʼ ἂν ἤδη . τὰ μακρὰ τῶν σμικρῶν λόγων
sections.translation: I will speak now . A long statement has advantages over a short one and is more intelligible to listen to . Give me nothing of your own , Menelaus , but repay what you received from my father . As Menelaus makes a deprecating gesture . I am not speaking of possessions ; if you save my life ,

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: σῴσῃς , ἅπερ μοι φίλτατʼ ἐστὶ τῶν ἐμῶν . —
sections.translation: you will save my dearest possession .

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: ἀδικῶ · λαβεῖν χρή μʼ ἀντὶ τοῦδε τοῦ κακοῦ
sections.translation: I have done wrong ; I ought to have a little wrong-doing from you to requite that evil , for my father Agamemnon also did wrong in gathering the Hellenes and going to Ilium , not that he had sinned himself ,

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: τῆς σῆς γυναικὸς ἀδικίαν τʼ ἰώμενος .
sections.translation: but he was trying to find a cure for the sin and wrong-doing of your wife . So this is one thing you are bound to pay me back . For he really gave his life , as friends should , toiling hard in battle with you , so that you might have your wife again .

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: ἀπότεισον οὖν μοι ταὐτὸ τοῦτʼ ἐκεῖ λαβών ,
sections.translation: Pay back to me the same thing you got there . For one day exert yourself , on my behalf standing up in my defense , not ten full years .

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: ἃ δʼ Αὐλὶς ἔλαβε σφάγιʼ ἐμῆς ὁμοσπόρου ,
sections.translation: As for what Aulis took , the sacrifice of my sister , I let you have that ; do not kill Hermione .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: δεῖ γὰρ σʼ ἐμοῦ πράσσοντος ὡς πράσσω τὰ νῦν
sections.translation: For in my present plight , you must have an advantage over me and I must pardon it . But give to my miserable father my life and the life of my sister , a maiden so long ; for by my death I shall leave my father ’ s house without an heir .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: ἐρεῖς · ἀδύνατον . αὐτὸ τοῦτο · τοὺς φίλους
sections.translation: You will say it is impossible . That ’ s the point ; friends are bound to help friends in trouble . But when fortune gives of its best , what need of friends ? For the god ’ s help is enough of itself when he is willing to give it .

sections.label: Section 669
sections.original: φιλεῖν δάμαρτα πᾶσιν Ἕλλησιν δοκεῖς ·
sections.translation: All Hellas believes that you love your wife ,

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: κοὐχ ὑποτρέχων σε τοῦτο θωπείᾳ λέγω ·
sections.translation: and I am not saying this to flatter or wheedle you ; by her I implore you .
